title: Mold and dampness watch list
summary: Practical guidance for “Mold and dampness watch list” within “Water · Sanitation · Health”. It supports safe collection, separation, treatment limits, storage, and conservation of water, produces a visible result, and states when to stop or move to a safer fallback.
content:
Practical guidance for “Mold and dampness watch list” within “Water · Sanitation · Health”. It supports safe collection, separation, treatment limits, storage, and conservation of water, produces a visible result, and states when to stop or move to a safer fallback.

Start with: Use a written trigger, one accountable owner, a visible result, and a scheduled review for this exact task. This is the opening check for “Mold and dampness watch list” in “Water · Sanitation · Health”.

Stop condition: Never assume clear-looking water is safe, and never mix unknown treatment chemicals. Apply this boundary to “Mold and dampness watch list” in “Water · Sanitation · Health”.

Reduced fallback: If “Mold and dampness watch list” cannot be completed, do only the first verified step, mark the remaining capability unavailable, and use a protected supply confirmed safe by the competent authority.

steps: Use a written trigger, one accountable owner, a visible result, and a scheduled review for this exact task. This is the opening check for “Mold and dampness watch list” in “Water · Sanitation · Health”.
Identify the source, likely contamination, intended use, and current official advisory. Record the result under “Water · Sanitation · Health” before continuing “Mold and dampness watch list”.
Keep raw, treated, and wastewater containers physically separate and clearly labelled. Recheck this point before closing “Mold and dampness watch list” for “Water · Sanitation · Health”.

Use only a treatment method appropriate to the suspected hazard; ordinary treatment does not remove every chemical or radioactive contaminant. Treat any uncertainty here as an unresolved item in “Mold and dampness watch list” for “Water · Sanitation · Health”.
Protect the treated supply from hands, cups, animals, sunlight, leaks, and dirty caps. A second person should be able to verify this part of “Mold and dampness watch list”.
Log the fill date, treatment, users, remaining volume, and next rotation or inspection. Include the outcome of “Mold and dampness watch list” in the handover for “Water · Sanitation · Health”.
warnings: Never assume clear-looking water is safe, and never mix unknown treatment chemicals. Apply this boundary to “Mold and dampness watch list” in “Water · Sanitation · Health”.
Do not use containers that previously held fuel, pesticides, solvents, or other toxic products. Apply this boundary to “Mold and dampness watch list” in “Water · Sanitation · Health”.
common_mistakes: Skipping the opening control makes “Mold and dampness watch list” unreliable: Use a written trigger, one accountable owner, a visible result, and a scheduled review for this exact task. Apply this boundary to “Mold and dampness watch list” in “Water · Sanitation · Health”.

Treating the result as safe while ignoring this limit is a serious error: Never assume clear-looking water is safe, and never mix unknown treatment chemicals. Apply this boundary to “Mold and dampness watch list” in “Water · Sanitation · Health”.

Failing to document the final handover action leaves the next person without a trustworthy starting point: Log the fill date, treatment, users, remaining volume, and next rotation or inspection. Apply this boundary to “Mold and dampness watch list” in “Water · Sanitation · Health”.
alternatives: If “Mold and dampness watch list” cannot be completed, do only the first verified step, mark the remaining capability unavailable, and use a protected supply confirmed safe by the competent authority.
Use a dated paper note and spoken read-back for “Mold and dampness watch list” in “Water · Sanitation · Health” when the normal form, device, or communication channel is unavailable.
failure_signs: Stop and reassess “Mold and dampness watch list” when treated water develops an unusual smell, colour, taste, sediment, damaged seal, or uncertain history. Apply this boundary to “Mold and dampness watch list” in “Water · Sanitation · Health”.

Stop and reassess “Mold and dampness watch list” when illness appears among users or the source becomes affected by floodwater, sewage, chemicals, or an official warning. Apply this boundary to “Mold and dampness watch list” in “Water · Sanitation · Health”.
when_not_to_use: Do not continue “Mold and dampness watch list” while breathing, alertness, circulation, major bleeding, severe pain, seizure, poisoning, chest symptoms, pregnancy danger signs, or rapid deterioration is present. Apply this boundary to “Mold and dampness watch list” in “Water · Sanitation · Health”.

This card is not authorization to diagnose, prescribe, change a dose, perform an invasive procedure, force care, or delay 112 and qualified medical help. long_term: Review “Mold and dampness watch list” after real use in “Water · Sanitation · Health”; replace failed materials, practise the handover, and update thresholds when official guidance or local conditions change.
if_method_fails: If the method described in “Mold and dampness watch list” gives no verifiable result, stop relying on it, isolate the uncertain output, record where it failed, and switch to a protected supply confirmed safe by the competent authority.
environment_notes: Adapt the guidance in “Mold and dampness watch list” for “Water · Sanitation · Health” to weather, lighting, crowding, accessibility, power, water, transport, and available skill without removing the stop condition or independent check.
